patch.value: GH-601: SDK mutation option bags and projected list items are Record<string,unknown> — typos and wrong types compile clean under strict

Public SDK mutation inputs are typed per-action (derived from flag/schema contracts, not hand-written) so field typos and wrong value types fail tsc under strict; projected list rows expose typed id/core fields; no runtime behavior change; typed surface covered by a type-level test (tsd or equivalent)

GH-601. dist/sdk/runtime.d.ts: PmActionOptions = Record<string, unknown> for mutation inputs; projected list rows equally untyped — field typos/wrong value types compile clean under strict and fail at runtime; listed-item id access not type-safe. Fix direction: derive per-action input types from the existing flag/schema contracts (single source, no hand-written duplicates) + typed projection rows; add type-level tests. Serves SDK-first (pm-usfg) consumer contract quality.
